speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Ah, young grovekeeper! The forest grows stronger with your care. What wisdom can I share today?

choices.label: Any advice for a new keeper?
Tell me about the valley's history

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Water your saplings regularly — it boosts their growth speed significantly. And plant near water tiles for even better results. Rain does the watering for you, if you're patient.

choices.label: How do I get trees to Old Growth?

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Explore beyond the grove! The forest trail leads to new clearings where rare species grow wild. Harvest mature trees for timber and sap — they're the lifeblood of our village.

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Long ago, a great Worldtree stood at the heart of this valley. Its roots connected every living thing. When it fell, the forest began to wither. That's why we need grovekeepers like you — to restore what was lost.

choices.label: How can I help restore the forest?

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Spring is the season of growth — everything flourishes. Summer is steady, a good time to tend and harvest. Autumn slows growth but brings the richest harvests. And winter... well, the forest sleeps. Use it to plan and trade.

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Old Growth takes time and dedication. Keep watering, protect from drought, and choose species suited to your biome. Easier species like White Oak reach Old Growth fastest. The difficult ones take longer but yield more.

choices.label: What do Old Growth trees give?

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Old Growth trees produce the finest resources. Their harvest yields are doubled, and some species reveal special properties — like the Ghost Birch, which glows with an ethereal light. Truly magnificent.

choices.label: I must see that for myself!

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Growth halts in winter, but evergreen species like Elder Pine and Ironbark are hardier. Use winter to harvest mature trees, trade resources, and prepare for spring's burst of growth.

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Head east through the forest trail to reach the Sunlit Clearing — Botanist Fern studies there. South leads to Ferndale Village where you'll find Hazel's trading post and Blossom's seed shop. The ancient forest to the far east holds the rarest species.

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: The North Woods are wild and untamed. Bramble keeps watch there — a gruff fellow, but he knows the weather like no one else. He can warn you about storms and droughts before they arrive.

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: The Worldtree was ancient beyond reckoning. Some say it was the first tree ever planted. When a great storm struck, it fell, and the valley lost its heart. But I believe... in time, a new Worldtree could grow. Perhaps you'll be the one to plant it.

choices.label: I'll find a way to plant a new one!

speaker: Elder Rowan
text: Plant many species, tend them well, and expand your grove. Every tree you grow strengthens the forest's spirit. When you've reached a mastery of the forest — level 25 or beyond — ancient powers may awaken. The prestige path is not for the faint-hearted.

speaker: Hazel
text: Welcome to my trading post! I've got the best rates in Grovekeeper Valley. What'll it be?

speaker: Hazel
text: I trade timber for sap, sap for fruit, and fruit for acorns. Better rates than you'll find anywhere else! The seasonal market changes prices too — keep an eye on that.

speaker: Hazel
text: Smart trading is all about timing. In spring, seed prices drop — stock up then. Summer boosts timber value. Autumn is harvest time — fruit and acorns are worth more. Winter? Sap becomes gold.

choices.label: What's your best deal right now?

speaker: Hazel
text: Spring: seed costs halved — buy everything! Summer: timber sells for 50% more. Autumn: fruit and acorns get a 50% bonus. Winter: sap is king, 50% extra value. Plan your harvests around the seasons and you'll never go broke.

speaker: Hazel
text: Well, I always give bonus rates on timber-to-sap and fruit-to-acorn trades. Regular traders charge more! If you bring me plenty of timber, I'll make sure you get your sap's worth.

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: Oh! A fellow nature enthusiast! I've been cataloguing every species in the valley. Would you help me with some research?

choices.label: What have you discovered so far?

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: I'm studying the growth patterns of different species. Plant at least 5 different species and grow one to Old Growth stage — that would give me incredible data! I'll make it worth your while.

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: For every species you grow to maturity, I'll share what I've learned about its properties. Complete my research goals and I'll give you rare seeds you can't find anywhere else!

choices.label: I need to think about it

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: The ancient forest to the southeast has Redwoods and Ironbarks — the hardiest species around. And if you ever find a Ghost Birch... well, those are truly special. They glow at night!

choices.label: Tell me more about Ghost Birch

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: I've documented 15 distinct species in the valley so far! Each has unique growth patterns, resource yields, and environmental preferences. Some only thrive near water, others prefer rocky soil. The diversity is astonishing!

choices.label: How do I discover new species?

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: Ghost Birch is a prestige species — only available to experienced grovekeepers who've proven their dedication. Its bark is silver-white and at night, the entire canopy emits a soft ethereal glow. My notes say it yields mystic sap with unusual properties.

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: The Crystal Oak is extraordinary — its leaves shift through prismatic colors with the seasons. In spring they're emerald, summer brings gold, autumn shows crimson, and winter reveals ice-blue crystalline structures. It's the second prestige species.

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: Oh, the Weeping Willow! Its cascading branches are so graceful. And scientifically, its sap production is remarkably efficient. Plant one near water and it practically takes care of itself.

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: Explore new zones — wild trees in the North Woods and Ancient Forest are species you might not have yet. You can also buy seeds from Blossom in the village. As you level up, rarer species become available!

speaker: Botanist Fern
text: Elder Rowan knows more about the prestige path than I do. But from what I've gathered, once you reach the highest levels and choose to start anew, the forest rewards your sacrifice with access to these legendary species.

speaker: Blossom
text: Hello, dear! Looking for something special to plant? I've collected seeds from all across the valley!

speaker: Blossom
text: Cherry Blossoms are simply gorgeous — petals float through the air when they're mature! Golden Apples are practical too — wonderful fruit yields. Take your pick!

speaker: Blossom
text: Match your species to the season! Plant fast-growers in spring to take advantage of the growth boost. Save difficult species for when you have the resources to water them consistently. And always keep a few seeds in reserve — you never know when you'll need them.

speaker: Blossom
text: Cherry Blossoms are my absolute favorite! Difficulty 3, so they need some care. But when they reach Sapling stage and beyond, pink petals cascade through the air. It's the most beautiful sight in the valley. They yield fruit and a little sap.

choices.label: I'll take some Cherry Blossom seeds!

speaker: Blossom
text: Golden Apples are wonderful producers! They're a bit tougher to grow — difficulty 4 — but the fruit harvest is incredible. One Old Growth Golden Apple can feed the whole village. They need consistent watering though.

speaker: Blossom
text: White Oak is the easiest — difficulty 1, grows like a weed! Silver Birch is fast too, lovely sap yields. For something with more character, try the Weeping Willow — it loves water tiles.

speaker: Blossom
text: Silver Birch is perfect for beginners! Low cost, grows quickly, and produces lovely sap. I always have plenty in stock. White Oak seeds are free too — can't beat that!

speaker: Bramble
text: Hmm? Oh, another keeper wandering the woods. I suppose you want to know about the weather? That's all anyone ever asks me.

choices.label: How did you learn to read weather?

speaker: Bramble
text: I can smell rain on the wind two days before it arrives. When the mushrooms in the grotto glow brighter, a storm is near. When the leaves curl inward, drought follows. Pay attention to the forest — it tells you everything.

choices.label: What should I do during rain?

speaker: Bramble
text: Four seasons, four moods. Spring brings life — everything grows fast and rain is common. Summer is hot and steady but drought threatens. Autumn cools the air and slows growth, but the harvests are rich. Winter... winter is harsh. Only the strongest trees endure.

choices.label: Which season is best for planting?

speaker: Bramble
text: I've lived in these woods longer than most trees have stood. When you spend decades watching the sky, you learn its language. My grandmother was a weather watcher too — she taught me to read the clouds before I could read words.

speaker: Bramble
text: Rain is the forest's gift! It waters all your trees automatically — saves your stamina. Growth speeds up during rain. Plant new seeds right before a storm for the best head start. Don't waste rain days harvesting — use them for planting!

speaker: Bramble
text: Drought is the enemy. Growth slows to a crawl, and young trees are vulnerable. Water your most precious trees first — don't waste stamina on what's already mature. Structures like the well can help protect nearby trees.

speaker: Bramble
text: Windstorms can damage young trees — saplings are most at risk. Ironbark and Redwood resist wind better than most. If you hear the wind howling, check your grove for damage afterward. Strong trees weather any storm.

speaker: Bramble
text: Spring, without question. The growth multiplier is 1.5x — nothing beats it. Plant your most difficult species in spring and water them well. By summer they'll be saplings. By autumn, mature. That's the rhythm of a good grovekeeper.

choices.label: I'll plan my planting around spring!

speaker: Bramble
text: Focus on evergreen species — Elder Pine, Ironbark, and Redwood handle winter better. Use the dormant season to harvest mature trees, trade resources, and save stamina. When spring comes, you'll be ready to plant aggressively.

speaker: Bramble
text: She used to say: 'The forest speaks to those who listen.' She could predict storms a week in advance. I'm not quite that good, but I'm getting there. Every season teaches me something new.

speaker: Bramble
text: Watch the weather overlay — it tells you what's happening. Rain means growth boost. Drought means danger. Wind means potential damage. Each event lasts a while, so plan your actions accordingly. That's lesson one.

speaker: Willow
text: Welcome to the hollow, friend. The waters here run deep and the plants grow strong. How can this old herbalist help you today?

choices.label: How do I keep my trees healthy?
What herbs do you work with?

speaker: Willow
text: Water is everything. A watered tree grows faster, yields more, and resists weather damage. Don't wait for rain — be proactive. And prune your trees when they're mature to boost their next growth cycle.

speaker: Willow
text: Mossy Hollow is one of the most fertile spots in the valley. The water table is high, and the soil is rich with nutrients. Trees planted here benefit from the natural moisture. I've been studying the moss — it has remarkable properties.

speaker: Willow
text: I collect sap from various species — each has unique qualities. Willow sap soothes, pine sap strengthens, and birch sap energizes. The forest is the greatest apothecary. If you bring me rare sap, I can teach you much.

speaker: Willow
text: Water every tree at least once between growth stages. The watering bonus increases growth speed by 30%. During drought, prioritize your youngest trees — they're the most vulnerable. Mature trees can weather a dry spell.

speaker: Willow
text: Pruning shears are unlocked at level 5. A well-pruned tree recovers faster and produces slightly more during its next harvest. Don't prune seeds or sprouts though — they need all the energy they can get.

speaker: Willow
text: This moss absorbs water and slowly releases it to nearby roots. Trees planted near moss patches grow slightly faster. It's nature's own irrigation system. The hollow has more moss than anywhere else in the valley.

choices.label: I'll plant my best trees here!

speaker: Oakley
text: Well, well! Another visitor to the workshop. I've been shaping wood since before you could hold a trowel. Need something built, upgraded, or crafted?

speaker: Oakley
text: I build structures that help your grove! Greenhouses boost growth speed in a radius. Storage sheds increase your resource capacity. Wells help protect trees from drought. Fences keep the aesthetics tidy. Bring me timber and I'll build anything.

choices.label: What's the best structure to start with?
How do structures affect my grove?

speaker: Oakley
text: Every tool in your belt can be improved! Better tools use less stamina and work more effectively. The trowel plants faster, the watering can covers more area, and the axe harvests more resources. Bring me materials and I'll upgrade them.

speaker: Oakley
text: Crafting turns raw resources into useful items. Timber becomes planks, sap becomes varnish, fruit becomes preserves. Each recipe requires specific ingredients and sometimes a nearby crafting station. Want me to show you what I know?

speaker: Oakley
text: Start with a well — it protects nearby trees from drought damage. Then build a greenhouse for the growth boost. After that, storage sheds let you stockpile more resources. Think of structures as investments in your grove's future.

speaker: Oakley
text: Each structure has an effect radius. Greenhouses boost growth of all trees within 3 tiles. Wells protect against drought in a 2-tile radius. Storage sheds increase your maximum resource storage. Place them strategically for maximum benefit!

speaker: Oakley
text: Basic upgrades need timber and sap. Advanced upgrades require fruit and acorns too. The most powerful upgrades need rare materials that only come from the deepest parts of the forest. Talk to Ember if you're looking for something truly special.

speaker: Oakley
text: Here are the essentials: Refine Timber — turns raw timber into planks. Press Fruit — makes preserves. Cultivate Seeds — turns acorns into planting seeds. Mill Timber — creates fine lumber for structures. Start with these and work your way up!

speaker: Oakley
text: Advanced recipes unlock as you level up and craft more. Growth potions, harvest boosters, stamina tonics — all possible with the right ingredients. Ember knows the really exotic stuff, though. Between the two of us, we can make anything.

speaker: Thorn
text: Hold, keeper. The ancient forest is not for the unprepared. I've patrolled these woods for years, and they still surprise me. What brings you to the deep woods?

speaker: Thorn
text: You've come to the right place. Redwoods and Ironbarks grow wild here — the toughest trees in the valley. Further in, I've spotted Baobab groves. Difficulty 5, but the timber yield is legendary. Want me to point you to the best specimens?

speaker: Thorn
text: The ancient forest is prone to windstorms — they sweep through without warning and can damage young trees. Drought hits hard here too, since the canopy blocks most rain from reaching the forest floor. Be prepared.

choices.label: How do I protect my trees?

speaker: Thorn
text: I could use a hand. The ancient forest needs rebalancing — too many of one species crowds out the rest. Plant diverse species in the clearings, and harvest overgrown areas to let new life in. Also, I need someone to map the rare species locations.

speaker: Thorn
text: Ironbark is the toughest species in the valley — difficulty 4, but nearly immune to wind damage. Its timber is the strongest and most valuable. Plant it in exposed areas where other trees would struggle. It thrives where others fail.

speaker: Thorn
text: Baobabs are ancient and massive — the widest trunks in the forest. Difficulty 5, but they store water internally and can survive drought better than anything. Their canopy provides shade for nearby trees. A single Old Growth Baobab is a grove unto itself.

speaker: Thorn
text: Build structures with protection effects near your vulnerable trees. Ironbark and Redwood resist wind naturally. During drought, focus your watering on the youngest trees. And always keep some stamina in reserve for emergencies.

speaker: Thorn
text: Complete my tasks and I'll share seeds from rare species I've collected on my patrols. I have Redwood and Ironbark seeds that aren't easy to come by. Help the forest, and the forest helps you.

speaker: Sage
text: Ah... the mushrooms told me you'd come. Welcome to the grotto, young keeper. I am Sage, keeper of the old knowledge. What truth do you seek?

choices.label: What are the mushrooms telling you?

speaker: Sage
text: The Worldtree was the axis of creation — its roots drank from the underground rivers, and its canopy touched the sky. Every species in this valley descended from its seeds. When it fell, the forest fragmented. But its legacy lives in every tree you plant.

speaker: Sage
text: When a grovekeeper reaches the height of mastery — level 25 — they face a choice. Surrender their progress and begin anew, carrying only wisdom and earning permanent blessings. Growth becomes faster, harvests richer, stamina greater. And the forest reveals species hidden from ordinary eyes.

speaker: Sage
text: The mushrooms are ancient communicators. They connect the roots of every tree through underground networks. When a tree is stressed, the mushrooms carry the signal. When rain approaches, they glow brighter. They are the forest's nervous system.

choices.label: Can I learn to read them?

speaker: Sage
text: Legends say that when a grovekeeper has walked the prestige path and planted every species in the valley, the Worldtree's seed will sprout once more. It's the ultimate goal — to heal the heart of the forest. Perhaps you will be the one.

speaker: Sage
text: A great blight, they say. Or perhaps the forest simply grew too old without renewal. The truth is lost to time. What matters now is not the falling, but the rising. Every seed you plant is a step toward restoration.

speaker: Sage
text: Three legendary species emerge from the prestige mist: Ghost Birch, whose bark glows in moonlight. Crystal Oak, whose leaves shift through prismatic colors with the seasons. And the Worldtree Sapling itself — but that requires the deepest commitment of all.

speaker: Sage
text: Each prestige cycle grants: 10% faster growth, 10% more XP, bonus stamina, and 5% better harvest yields. These stack with each prestige. The forest also grants cosmetic blessings — your grove's border transforms with each cycle, from Stone Wall to Ancient Runes.

choices.label: The rewards are worth the sacrifice!

speaker: Sage
text: It takes years to fully understand, but here's a start: bright mushrooms mean rain is near. Dull mushrooms mean dry weather. Red caps mean windstorm. And when they pulse with a golden light... the forest is content. You'll learn to see it in time.

speaker: Ember
text: Ah-ha! A visitor! Welcome to my outdoor laboratory. I'm Ember — alchemist, experimenter, and collector of the unusual. Are you here about potions, rare ingredients, or something truly extraordinary?

speaker: Ember
text: I brew all manner of tonics! Growth Elixir speeds up tree development. Harvest Potion boosts yields for a full day. Stamina Tonic restores your energy. And my latest creation — the Rain Caller — can summon a brief shower!

choices.label: How do I make Growth Elixir?

speaker: Ember
text: The rarest ingredients come from the forest itself! Amber drops from ancient trees at dawn. Heartwood appears in trees that have been lovingly watered their entire life. Mystic Sap flows from Ghost Birch. Crystal Bark flakes off Crystal Oaks. And Ancient Seeds... well, those are the rarest of all.

speaker: Ember
text: Oh, you want the REALLY good stuff? I've been working on a formula that could... theoretically... accelerate an entire grove's growth for a full season. But I'd need 10 Amber, 5 Heartwood, and a Crystal Bark. Not exactly easy to come by!

speaker: Ember
text: Growth Elixir needs 5 sap, 3 fruit, and 1 Amber. Mix them at a crafting station and voila — pour it on any tree to boost its growth speed for several growth stages. It's my most popular brew!

speaker: Ember
text: The Rain Caller is my pride and joy! 3 Mystic Sap, 5 regular sap, and 2 fruit. Use it and a brief rain shower waters every tree in your current zone. Perfect for drought emergencies. The formula took me three years to perfect.

speaker: Ember
text: Amber forms slowly within Old Growth trees. Harvest an Old Growth tree at dawn and you have a small chance of finding it. Certain species — like Flame Maple and Redwood — produce amber more frequently. Patience is key.

speaker: Ember
text: Ancient Seeds are the rarest material in the valley. They only appear in Old Growth trees that have survived all four seasons. The seed carries the memory of every season it endured. With enough of them, you could craft something truly legendary.

choices.label: I'll grow the oldest trees possible!

speaker: Ember
text: Nothing is impossible for a dedicated grovekeeper! Start small — grow Old Growth trees, harvest at the right times, and collect rare materials gradually. Rome wasn't built in a day, and neither is a master alchemist's stockpile!
